name: Vietnamese Imperial Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Small dried tree ear mushrooms
1 Garlic clove, squeezed through a garlic press
1 Red chile (fresh or dried), seeded and finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the tree ear mushrooms and bean thread noodles in warm water until soft, about 6 minutes.
2. Drain the mushrooms and remove any hard centers. Coarsely chop them.
3. Drain the noodles and chop into 2-inch lengths.
4. Mix pork, garlic, shallots, fish sauce, and pepper. Add mushrooms, noodles, carrots, and bean sprouts; mix together with your hands.
5. Combine garlic, sugar, lime juice, fish sauce, chile, and water for the dipping sauce. Let sit at room temperature for at least 10 minutes.
6. To form rolls: Dip a wrapper in warm water and sugar mixture until slightly softened. Place it flat on a counter or towel.
7. Shape filling into a tight log (1-inch diameter x 4-5 inches long). Place along the bottom third of the wrapper.
8. Roll up to seal, ensuring the wrapper is taut around the filling. Fold outside edges inward and roll up to seal.
9. Place rolls seam side down on an oiled baking sheet covered with a damp towel. Make remaining rolls similarly.
10. Heat 2 inches of oil in a wok or deep fryer to 325°F. Add a few rolls at a time, frying for 10 seconds. Increase heat to 375°F and continue frying until golden brown (6-8 minutes).
11. Remove rolls to paper towels to drain.
12. Serve by cutting fried rolls into crosswise pieces, dipping into sauce, and enjoying as an appetizer or wrapping in lettuce leaves with coriander and mint.
Makes 16 rolls
Per roll: 175 calories, 5g protein, 18g carbohydrate, 8g fat (3g saturated), 1mg cholesterol, 90mg sodium, 1g fiber.
